name: nika-spec
general_comments: Machine-readable self-assessment for the OpenSSF Best Practices (passing) badge. The authoritative, human-readable version with evidence links is governance/certifications.md, section 'OpenSSF Best Practices'. Only criteria this project can answer honestly are recorded here; criteria left unset are intentionally omitted.
description_good_justification: The README's opening states what Nika is (a workflow-language specification) and the problem it solves.
interact_justification: The public can interact through GitHub issues, pull requests, and discussions.
floss_license_justification: Released under Apache-2.0, an OSI-approved and FSF-free software license (LICENSE at repository root).
license_location_justification: The LICENSE file is at the repository top level; REUSE.toml declares Apache-2.0 for every file (reuse lint green).
documentation_basics_justification: The README plus the normative spec/ tree document the language and its conformance model.
documentation_interface_justification: spec/ documents the external interface (the nika: v1 envelope, verbs, schema); the published JSON schema documents editor/CI integration.
sites_https_justification: The repository, its tracker, and referenced sites are served over HTTPS (github.com, bestpractices.dev, schemastore).
discussion_justification: GitHub issues and pull requests provide public discussion of proposed changes.
english_justification: Documentation, issues, and commit messages are written in English.
maintained_justification: The project is actively maintained: recent commits, tagged releases, and a responsive tracker.
repo_public_justification: The source repository is publicly readable on GitHub.
repo_track_justification: Git records every change with its author, date, and message.
repo_interim_justification: Interim development versions are available between releases via the public git history (main branch).
repo_distributed_justification: Git is a distributed version-control system.
version_unique_justification: Each release carries a unique version identifier (VERSION file plus git tags).
release_notes_justification: CHANGELOG.md records human-readable release notes per version (Keep-a-Changelog).
report_process_justification: SECURITY.md and the GitHub issue tracker document how to report bugs and vulnerabilities.
report_tracker_justification: GitHub Issues is the public bug tracker.
report_archive_justification: The GitHub issue tracker preserves an archive of reports and their discussion.
vulnerability_report_process_justificati: SECURITY.md documents the vulnerability reporting process (email security@supernovae.studio plus GitHub private advisories).
test_justification: The conformance suite (conformance/runner.py plus the per-domain *_selftest.py files) is an automated test suite over every shipped artifact.
test_invocation_justification: conformance/run.sh and `python conformance/runner.py all` invoke the full suite from the repository root; conformance/README.md documents it.
test_continuous_integration_justificatio: The Conformance workflow (.github/workflows/conformance.yml) runs the suite on every push and pull request.
delivery_mitm_justification: Software and docs are delivered over HTTPS (git over TLS/SSH), countering man-in-the-middle tampering.
no_leaked_credentials_justification: No credentials are committed; the repository carries a specification and its tooling only, and hygiene gates screen the tree.
know_secure_design_justification: The maintainers apply secure-design principles: least-privilege CI, no untrusted input in shell steps, SHA-pinned actions.
know_common_errors_justification: The maintainers understand common implementation errors; the conformance oracle statically analyzes every artifact for spec-conformance defects.
vulnerabilities_fixed_60_days_justificat: There are no known unpatched vulnerabilities; none have been reported to date.
contribution_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md explains how to contribute, including the rule that every normative change ships a conformance corpus case.
contribution_requirements_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md states the requirements for acceptable contributions.
test_policy_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md's rule (every normative change ships a conformance corpus case) is the project's test policy.
tests_are_added_justification: New normative behaviour is added together with its conformance corpus case, enforced by the static gate.
vulnerability_report_private_justificati: GitHub private vulnerability reporting is enabled on the repository; SECURITY.md cites GitHub Security Advisories as the preferred private channel (email fallback: security@supernovae.studio).
static_analysis_justification: Ruff (config in ruff.toml) runs on every push and pull request via the Conformance workflow, and CodeQL (.github/workflows/codeql.yml) runs security static analysis of the Python tooling; the conformance oracle additionally analyzes every shipped artifact against the spec.
crypto_published_justification: No cryptographic mechanisms are shipped by this specification-plus-tooling repository.
crypto_call_justification: No cryptographic mechanisms are shipped by this specification-plus-tooling repository.
crypto_floss_justification: No cryptographic mechanisms are shipped by this specification-plus-tooling repository.
crypto_keylength_justification: No cryptographic mechanisms are shipped by this specification-plus-tooling repository.
crypto_working_justification: No cryptographic mechanisms are shipped by this specification-plus-tooling repository.
crypto_weaknesses_justification: No cryptographic mechanisms are shipped by this specification-plus-tooling repository.
crypto_pfs_justification: No cryptographic mechanisms are shipped by this specification-plus-tooling repository.
crypto_password_storage_justification: No cryptographic mechanisms are shipped by this specification-plus-tooling repository.
crypto_random_justification: No cryptographic mechanisms are shipped by this specification-plus-tooling repository.
build_justification: No build step exists; the Markdown specification and Python scripts run in place.
build_common_tools_justification: No build process exists (no compiled artifacts).
dynamic_analysis_unsafe_justification: No memory-unsafe language is used; the tooling is Python only.
release_notes_vulns_justification: No publicly-known vulnerabilities have been fixed to date, so there are none to enumerate in release notes.
